Water Heater Repair in Chicago, IL

Licensed Plumbing Professionals • Available 24/7

Call 833-758-6911
Home › Services › Water Heater Repair
Licensed & Insured 24/7 Service Serving Chicagoland

Water Heater Repair

No hot water in a Chicago winter is an emergency. We diagnose and repair gas and electric water heaters — tank and tankless — throughout Chicagoland. Most repairs can be done the same day, and we carry the common parts on our trucks.

Common Water Heater Problems

  • No hot water: Dead pilot light, failed thermocouple, bad heating element, or tripped reset
  • Not enough hot water: Sediment buildup, undersized tank, or a failing dip tube
  • Water too hot: Thermostat set too high or a malfunctioning thermostat
  • Leaking tank: Could be a relief valve, a fitting, or the tank itself (tank corrosion = replacement)
  • Strange noises: Popping or rumbling usually means sediment buildup on the bottom of the tank
  • Rusty or smelly water: Corroded anode rod or bacteria in the tank

Typical Water Heater Repair Costs

  • Thermocouple replacement: $100–$200
  • Heating element replacement (electric): $150–$300
  • Thermostat replacement: $150–$300
  • T&P relief valve replacement: $100–$250
  • Anode rod replacement: $150–$300
  • Dip tube replacement: $150–$300
  • Tank flush (sediment removal): $100–$200

If your water heater is over 10 years old and the tank is leaking, replacement is usually more practical than repair. We'll give you an honest assessment.

Frequently Asked Questions

How long do water heaters last?

Tank water heaters typically last 8–12 years. Tankless units can last 15–20 years with proper maintenance. Hard water (common in Chicago suburbs) shortens lifespan due to mineral buildup.

Why is my water heater making popping sounds?

Sediment has settled on the bottom of the tank. When the burner heats the water, it creates steam bubbles under the sediment layer. A tank flush can solve this. If it's been years since a flush, the sediment may be hardite and the tank may need replacement.

Can a water heater be repaired if it's leaking from the bottom?

If the leak is from the drain valve or a fitting, yes. If the tank itself is leaking (internal corrosion), no — the tank can't be patched. Replacement is the only option.

Should I repair or replace my water heater?

If the unit is under 8 years old and the repair is under $400, repair makes sense. If it's over 10, or the tank is corroding, invest in a new one. You'll get better efficiency and a fresh warranty.

Do you repair both gas and electric water heaters?

Yes. We work on all major brands — Rheem, AO Smith, Bradford White, State, and others. Gas and electric, tank and tankless.

Need Water Heater Repair?

Our licensed plumbers are available 24/7 for emergency and scheduled service.
